text.title: AI-Assisted Lessons-Learned Synthesis Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Every theme MUST cite ≥ 2 verbatim quotes from source retros with source_doc + section + line range. Paraphrased summaries without quotes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: LLMs trained to summarize paraphrase even when instructed not to; verbatim citation forces the model to extract real text. Without citations, the synthesis is plausible-sounding fiction.

rule.statement: A theme MUST appear in ≥ 2 source retros to qualify for the main synthesis. Single-mention insights move to a 'long tail' appendix.
rule.rationale: The 2-source minimum prevents the LLM from imagining plausible themes that no real retro mentioned ('communication issues' applies to every project).

rule.statement: Each theme has two scores: severity (0-3 from cited language) and recurrence (count of distinct retros). Themes ranked by severity × recurrence.

rationale.source: Pareto analysis applied to retro corpora

rule.rationale: Severity alone over-ranks emotional retros; recurrence alone misses one-time catastrophes. The product captures both and surfaces the right top-K.

rule.statement: Synthesis MUST propose 5-10 action items, each with named owner role (not 'team'), deadline ≤ 1 quarter, success criteria.
rule.rationale: Anonymous action items don't get done. Named role + deadline turns insight into commitment; 5-10 fits a quarter without dilution.

rule.statement: Agent output is a DRAFT. A human (PM, delivery lead) validates 100% of citations against sources, edits language, and signs the final version.
rule.rationale: LLM citation errors run 5-15% even with grounding; without verification the synthesis carries unearned authority.

rule.statement: If preconditions fail (e.g. < 5 retros, retros not written, HR-sensitive content), the agent MUST stop and route the request elsewhere.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology on too small a sample yields confident-sounding noise; honest skip preserves trust.
